ancient shell.name: ancient shell
ancient shell.description: a fragment of resilient tortodrone shell. it is rumored that by sleeping on a tortodrone shell, one can feel the subtle vibrations that call these mighty beings towards the long migration's mysterious final destination.
ancient shell.notes: this material was introduced with release 2014-02-26 during the first march of the tortodrones event. all related equipment requires a total of 28 ancient shells to fully create a five-star handgun or shield. this means that in order to craft every five-star piece of equipment, it would take 224 ancient shells.

beast scale.name: beast scale

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 1 and below

beast scale.description: a relatively intact scale recovered from a clockworks creature.
beast scale.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

binding essence.name: binding essence
binding essence.description: a strange, vile reagent thought to be harnessed by the apocrea to build their structures. once it gets on your skin it seems to never wash off. no, it must, you simply need to scrub harder. harder, yes...
binding essence.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-10-23 during the first shroud of the apocrea event. its description is written in the style of lovecraftian horror .

blast powder.name: blast powder

dropped_by: any defeated fire themed monster in depth 4 and below

blast powder.description: a dangerous by-product produced by fire-based organic creatures. gremlins like to use it to blow stuff up.
blast powder.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

blaze pepper.name: blaze pepper

dropped_by: any defeated fire themed monster in depth 13 and below

blaze pepper.description: a delectable spice prized by clockworks denizens of all sorts. in powdered form, it has a terrible habit of clinging to absolutely everything.
blaze pepper.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched. the description of this item used to be "a delectable spice hoarded by clockworks beasts in anticipation of a winter that never comes. in powdered form, it has a terrible habit of clinging to absolutely everything." it was changed after someone suggested on the forums that it was inappropriate after the update in which freeze themed monsters were added. 1

blighted bone.name: blighted bone

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 13 and below

blighted bone.description: a peculiar patella that's seen better days, after having seen a few better knights.
blighted bone.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

blue shard.name: blue shard

dropped_by: any defeated construct or gremlin in depth 1 and below

blue shard.description: a shard of moonstone that is too small to be used in powering gates but highly useful in alchemy.
blue shard.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

book of dark rituals.name: book of dark rituals
book of dark rituals.description: an ancient grimoire filled with unspeakable eldritch rites. just like any cursed tome, there is likely some twisted individual out there who wants it for extremely nefarious purposes. trade with caution.
book of dark rituals.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-01-30 during the first kataclysmic confrontation . it is more like a key than a material, but it is stored in the material section of the player's arsenal. it is suggested that a book be used with four people in the party to maximize this rare object's use.

bottomless stocking.name: bottomless stocking
bottomless stocking.description: an interdimensional tear in this fuzzy fabric sock swallows any item placed within it. it even swallowed the stitches.
bottomless stocking.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-12-11 during the third winterfest.

brimstone.name: brimstone

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 1 and below

brimstone.description: a smelly, stygian, sulfuric shard commonly found in the underworld.
brimstone.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

bronze bolt.name: bronze bolt

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 1 and below

bronze bolt.description: a simple bronze bolt found everywhere throughout the clockworks.
bronze bolt.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

brute core.name: brute core

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 8 and below

brute core.description: an oozing blue lump from a particularly potent gelatinous opponent.
brute core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

bushy tail.name: bushy tail

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 8 and below

bushy tail.description: a peculiarly intact specimen of a clockworks beast's tail.
bushy tail.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

celestial ore.name: celestial ore
celestial ore.description: an extremely rare metal of great power said to be from the heavens above. it is often completely wasted on crowns and jewelry for slime royalty.
celestial ore.notes: this material was introduced with release 2011-10-19 .

chroma tear.name: chroma tear

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 13 and below

chroma tear.description: a crystallized chromalisk tear suitable as a lens for focusing weaponry, or for correcting myopia.
chroma tear.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

compact supply pack.name: compact supply pack
compact supply pack.description: a package of useful supplies valued by spiral knight research teams working deep within the clockworks. this compact pack has enough supplies to last a single knight one week.
compact supply pack.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-02-22 . it seems to function as a story item, keeping players mindful of the world around them.

cooling cell.name: cooling cell

dropped_by: any defeated freeze themed monster in depth 4 and below

cooling cell.description: a small device used in complex machines to keep them from overheating. also handy to keep in your pockets on hot summer days.
cooling cell.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

corrosive acid.name: corrosive acid

dropped_by: any defeated poison themed monster in depth 13 and below

corrosive acid.description: a caustic substance characteristic of corrosive creatures.
corrosive acid.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

crest of ur.name: crest of ur

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 18 and below

crest of ur.description: a crest from the lost legion of ur, an elite band of fiendish warrior said to have once conquered the underworld. whomever holds it can faintly hear the sound of drums and the clomping of hooves.
crest of ur.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

critter carapace.name: critter carapace

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 4 and below

critter carapace.description: a chitinous chunk carefully shucked from a clockworks creature. it's in surprisingly good shape.
critter carapace.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

dark ember.name: dark ember
dark ember.description: a mote of burning energy salvaged from lord vanaduke's throne room. it slowly smolders with a sinister intensity, creating an almost hypnotic trance when gazed upon for any length of time.
dark ember.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-12-12 .

dark shard.name: dark shard

dropped_by: any defeated fiend or undead in depth 1 and below

dark shard.description: a shard of dark matter that is too small to be used in powering gates but highly useful in alchemy.
dark shard.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

dead gold.name: dead gold

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 8 and below

dead gold.description: a coin placed into the hands of the dead before burial, granting them fare for the long journey ahead.
dead gold.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

deluxe supply pack.name: deluxe supply pack
deluxe supply pack.description: a package of useful supplies valued by spiral knight research teams working deep within the clockworks. this compact pack has enough supplies to last an entire squad of knights one week.
deluxe supply pack.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-02-22 . it seems to function as a story item, keeping players mindful of the world around them.

divine dust.name: divine dust
divine dust.description: a portion of special food favored by battle sprites who forever bask in the light of the heavens.
divine dust.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

divine mote.name: divine mote
divine mote.description: a morsel of special food favored by battle sprites who forever bask in the light of the heavens.
divine mote.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

divine orb.name: divine orb
divine orb.description: a lustrous sphere of special food favored by battle sprites who forever bask in the light of the heavens.
divine orb.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

divine star.name: divine star
divine star.description: a perfect crystalization of special food favored by battle sprites who forever bask in the light of the heavens.
divine star.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

divine stone.name: divine stone
divine stone.description: a concentrated lump of special food favored by battle sprites who forever bask in the light of the heavens.
divine stone.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

dragon scale.name: dragon scale

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 18 and below

dragon scale.description: an armored scale from what was thought to be an extinct creature. it reflects light with an otherworldly sheen.
dragon scale.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

drake scale.name: drake scale

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 8 and below

drake scale.description: a solid scale wrested from a brutal beast.
drake scale.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

driftwood.name: driftwood

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 4 and below

driftwood.description: a peculiar piece of wood that just won't stay put.
driftwood.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

ecto drop.name: ecto drop

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 1 and below

ecto drop.description: a bit of ectoplasm meticulously collected from an unwilling undead volunteer. it's an unpleasant shade of green.
ecto drop.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

enamorock.name: enamorock
enamorock.description: a strange stone that makes you have warm and fuzzy feelings inside just by holding it.
enamorock.notes: this material was introduced with release 2011-12-12 .

everfrost.name: everfrost

dropped_by: any defeated freeze themed monster in depth 18 and below

everfrost.description: a small patch of ice crystals that never seem to melt, even when exposed to direct heat.
everfrost.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched. in the dangerous mission: heart of ice it is found via memos from morgral'm that maulos requested and later stole a "pillar of everfrost."

fiendish amu glyph.name: fiendish amu glyph
fiendish amu glyph.description: a fragment of a fiendish id card. combining five different glyphs into an id card will allow you to open the grim gate in the ancient grove.
fiendish amu glyph.notes: this material was introduced with release 2014-02-26 during the first march of the tortodrones event. amu , nok , sol , tor , and ur are related to each other regarding the id card as well as in name. there are relationships between groups of fiends hinted at elsewhere in cradle . examples include "amu-sol" in the dangerous mission: heart of ice and devilite plans for trojans in the 8-1 mission: the return of ur .

fiendish id card.name: fiendish id card
fiendish id card.description: using this will allow you to open and pass through the grim gate in the ancient grove.
fiendish id card.notes: this material was introduced with release 2014-02-26 during the first march of the tortodrones event. it is more like a key than a material, but it is stored in the material section of the player's arsenal. it and sprite food are the only materials in the game that have a crafting recipe. it is suggested that a card be used with four people in the party to maximize this somewhat expensive object's use.

fine fabric.name: fine fabric

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 8 and below

fine fabric.description: a well-made length of sturdy cloth, swiped from a clockworks inhabitant.
fine fabric.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

flame soul.name: flame soul

dropped_by: any defeated fire themed monster in depth 18 and below

flame soul.description: a powerful essence that left behind when a creature perishes during a blind, fiery rage.
flame soul.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

flawed seerus mask fragment.name: flawed seerus mask fragment
flawed seerus mask fragment.description: a flawed fragment of the mask once worn by warmaster seerus, recovered from the siege of his grand arsenal.
flawed seerus mask fragment.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-02-29 .

forbidden fruit.name: forbidden fruit

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 4 and below

forbidden fruit.description: a sinful snack favored by famished fiends.
forbidden fruit.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

force dynamo.name: force dynamo

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 8 and below

force dynamo.description: a beguiling gadget that can seemingly generate energy from nowhere.
force dynamo.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

fractured seerus mask fragment.name: fractured seerus mask fragment
fractured seerus mask fragment.description: a fractured fragment of the mask once worn by warmaster seerus, recovered from the siege of his grand arsenal.
fractured seerus mask fragment.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-02-29 .

frayed fabric.name: frayed fabric

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 4 and below

frayed fabric.description: the worn remains of a clockworks denizen's duds. it could probably be recycled.
frayed fabric.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

frost gel.name: frost gel

dropped_by: any defeated freeze themed monster in depth 4 and below

frost gel.description: a drop of chilly gunk collected from a cool clockworks customer.
frost gel.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

fuel canister.name: fuel canister

dropped_by: any defeated fire themed monster in depth 4 and below

fuel canister.description: a small receptacle once used to hold fuel for a complex machine.
fuel canister.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

gel core.name: gel core

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 4 and below

gel core.description: a fist-sized, gelatinous lump collected from a jelly.
gel core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

gel drop.name: gel drop

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 1 and below

gel drop.description: a drop of goo collected from a jelly. it's still quivering.
gel drop.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

ghost bell.name: ghost bell

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 13 and below

ghost bell.description: a small silvery bell worn by considerate ghosts that alerts the living when they are nearby.
ghost bell.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

grave soil.name: grave soil

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 4 and below

grave soil.description: dreadful dirt dredged from a creepy crypt. it is always a little cold to the touch.
grave soil.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

green shard.name: green shard

dropped_by: any defeated beast or slime in depth 1 and below

green shard.description: a shard of valestone that is too small to be used in powering gates, but highly useful in alchemy.
green shard.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

gremlin gizmo.name: gremlin gizmo

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 4 and below

gremlin gizmo.description: a strange device used in gremlin machinery. it is inscribed with a warning against exposing it to direct sunlight or submerging it in water.
gremlin gizmo.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

gremlonium.name: gremlonium
gremlonium.description: a highly advanced compound created by gremlin engineers to power their most devious machines. it constantly emits an unsettling hissing sound.
gremlonium.notes: this material was introduced with release 2011-10-19 .

hailstone.name: hailstone

dropped_by: any defeated freeze themed monster in depth 8 and below

hailstone.description: a small rock that's always a little cold to the touch. it's great for chilling beverages without diluting their flavor.
hailstone.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

heavy gear.name: heavy gear

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 13 and below

heavy gear.description: a heavily weighted gear that that once assisted in the operation of an enormous machine of some sort.
heavy gear.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

indestructible giftwrap.name: indestructible giftwrap
indestructible giftwrap.description: presents wrapped with this grinchlin gag can never be opened again. leave it to grinchlins to ruin complimentary gift wrapping services for everyone.
indestructible giftwrap.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-12-11 during the third winterfest.

iron gear.name: iron gear

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 4 and below

iron gear.description: a metal gear that was once a component of a complicated contraption.
iron gear.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

ironwood.name: ironwood

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 13 and below

ironwood.description: a pristine log of one of the hardest substances known to the spiral knights.
ironwood.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

jelly glue.name: jelly glue
jelly glue.description: a viscous compound developed by biotechs to allow for the study of slime creatures. when mixed with slime and jelly matter, it allows for reshaping without losing its form. though reserved for scientific purposes, the process is generally regarded as 'super fun'.
jelly glue.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-12-12 .

kat tribe fetish.name: kat tribe fetish
kat tribe fetish.description: a tattered fetish from the now extinct kat tribe, the ferocious feline warriors responsible for the destruction of the owlites and their prestigious high academy of magicks. the fetishes were carried in to battle by kat warriors and are said to bring good luck despite the ill fate of the kat tribe itself.
kat tribe fetish.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-01-30 during the first kataclysmic confrontation .

light shard.name: light shard

dropped_by: any defeated construct , slime , or undead in depth 1 and below

light shard.description: a shard of luminite that is too small to be used in powering gates but highly useful in alchemy.
light shard.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

lump of coal.name: lump of coal
lump of coal.description: a grinchlin gift for those who have been naughty or nice. grinchlins are equal opportunity party poopers.
lump of coal.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-12-11 during the third winterfest.

maiden's tear.name: maiden's tear
maiden's tear.description: a sacred jewel cherished by almirian knights. given to them by their beloved, it is said to keep them safe in battle.
maiden's tear.notes: this material was introduced with release 2011-10-19 .

meteor mote.name: meteor mote

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 13 and below

meteor mote.description: a small piece of a rare material that is said to have fallen from the sky. gremlins typically use it in the construction of power sources.
meteor mote.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

miracloth.name: miracloth

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 13 and below

miracloth.description: a marvelous bolt of sublimely supple fabric.
miracloth.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

mod calibrator.name: mod calibrator
mod calibrator.description: this complicated device was specially built to calibrate mods, and should not be confused with a calibrator mod, which modifies calibrators. it's a common mistake.
mod calibrator.notes: this material was introduced with release 2011-08-02 . its pricing increased from 50 to 75 tokens with release 2011-09-06 . calibrator mod, mentioned in this item's description, do not exist in-game. due to its primary method of acquisition, this material is heavily associated with the coliseum .

monster bone.name: monster bone

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 4 and below

monster bone.description: an intact bone from a now-deceased beast.
monster bone.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

mug of misery.name: mug of misery

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 13 and below

mug of misery.description: a cursed drinking utility that can only be used by a truly wicked soul. those that possess it are said to bring grave misfortune to those around them.
mug of misery.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

nightmare mane.name: nightmare mane
nightmare mane.description: a coat of coarse fur taken from a snarbolax. each fiber seems to curl and fray as if life is still flowing through it.
nightmare mane.notes: this material was introduced with release 2011-10-19 .

nightshade.name: nightshade

dropped_by: any defeated poison themed monster in depth 4 and below

nightshade.description: a hazardous herb that's bad for most beasts, but tasty to a teeming variety of toxic creatures.
nightshade.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

obsidian shard.name: obsidian shard
obsidian shard.description: a jet-black shard of unnatural stone. holding it fills your head with imagery of a distant shore where waves crash against rocks that seem to writhe in the chill of the water.
obsidian shard.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-10-23 during the first shroud of the apocrea event. its description is written in the style of lovecraftian horror .

owlite feather.name: owlite feather

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 4 and below

owlite feather.description: a small feather plucked from an owlite, said to be a mythical race of great magician birds.
owlite feather.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

perfect seerus mask fragment.name: perfect seerus mask fragment
perfect seerus mask fragment.description: a perfect fragment of the mask once worn by warmaster seerus, recovered from the siege of his grand arsenal.
perfect seerus mask fragment.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-02-29 .

perfect snowball.name: perfect snowball

dropped_by: any defeated freeze themed monster in depth 13 and below

perfect snowball.description: an icy snowball of almost mythical perfection. though it begs to be thrown squarely into the side of someone's head, it would be best to use in alchemy.
perfect snowball.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

perplexing element.name: perplexing element
perplexing element.description: a rare metal recovered from a shattered mountain deep within the realm of the apocrea. under a microscope it reveals to have a kind of impossible geometry. perhaps more dedicated study, free from the distractions of the world would reveal its secrets.
perplexing element.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-10-23 during the first shroud of the apocrea event. its description is written in the style of lovecraftian horror .

phial of phear.name: phial of phear

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 8 and below

phial of phear.description: a glass tube containing a small dose of phearium, a strange chemical said to induce panic into anyone who even gets so much as a whiff of the stuff.
phial of phear.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

philoso feather.name: philoso feather

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 18 and below

philoso feather.description: a large, grey feather plucked from the greatest of the owlite magicians.
philoso feather.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

plague essence.name: plague essence

dropped_by: any defeated poison themed monster in depth 18 and below

plague essence.description: a powerful essence left behind when a creature perishes from a particularly putrid disease.
plague essence.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

plasma cell.name: plasma cell

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 18 and below

plasma cell.description: a high-tech plasma source that could positively blind someone with science.
plasma cell.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched. its description alludes to the 1982 pop song " she blinded me with science ".

power cell.name: power cell

dropped_by: any defeated shock themed monster in depth 4 and below

power cell.description: a small device used to power complex machines.
power cell.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

power dust.name: power dust
power dust.description: a portion of special food favored by battle sprites who align themselves with forces that grant them brute strength on the battlefield.
power dust.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

power mote.name: power mote
power mote.description: a morsel of special food favored by battle sprites who align themselves with forces that grant them brute strength on the battlefield.
power mote.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

power orb.name: power orb
power orb.description: a lustrous sphere of special food favored by battle sprites who align themselves with forces that grant them brute strength on the battlefield.
power orb.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

power star.name: power star
power star.description: a perfect crystalization of special food favored by battle sprites who align themselves with forces that grant them brute strength on the battlefield.
power star.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

power stone.name: power stone
power stone.description: a concentrated lump of special food favored by battle sprites who align themselves with forces that grant them brute strength on the battlefield.
power stone.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

primal ore.name: primal ore

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 18 and below

primal ore.description: an extraordinary metal imbued with the savage spirit of the wild.
primal ore.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

punkin sprout.name: punkin sprout
punkin sprout.description: a small sprig capable of growing into a grand gourd.
punkin sprout.notes: this material was introduced 2014.29 22 october 2014 during the fourth dark harvest festival event.

razor ribbon.name: razor ribbon
razor ribbon.description: remember: when handling grinchlin giftboxes, always wear your plate mail gauntlets.
razor ribbon.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-12-11 during the third winterfest.

reaper rib.name: reaper rib

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 18 and below

reaper rib.description: a bone stained black by the wickedness of its former possessor.
reaper rib.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

red shard.name: red shard

dropped_by: any defeated beast , fiend , or gremlin in depth 1 and below

red shard.description: a shard of crimsonite that is too small to be used in powering gates but highly useful in alchemy.
red shard.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

redwood.name: redwood

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 8 and below

redwood.description: a sturdy chunk of fine, unfinished wood. it's typically used in the production of cabinets.
redwood.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

reset star.name: reset star
reset star.description: a special sprite food that resets applied skill points and reverts any ultimate skill selection.
reset star.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

rocket sprocket.name: rocket sprocket

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 8 and below

rocket sprocket.description: a sturdy sprocket used by machines that fire projectiles.
rocket sprocket.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

rock salt.name: rock salt
rock salt.description: a small amount of clockworks mineral that's been infused with absolutely nothing. it hungrily draws at nearby essences and flavors, making it a staple in cooking, and the bane of jellies everywhere.
rock salt.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched. similar to real-world salt and animals such as the slug , slimes are harmed by this material. this phenomenon is referenced in its description as well as the prestige mission: a pinch of salt . based on its description, this material seems to be a colorless mineral in material form.

rocky core.name: rocky core

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 13 and below

rocky core.description: a delicious gummy treat with an impenetrable rocky shell. more than a few teeth have been lost by knights attempting to get at the gooey interior.
rocky core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

royal core.name: royal core

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 18 and below

royal core.description: a sweet-smelling lump of ooze collected from a particularly noble specimen of slime.
royal core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

royal skewer.name: royal skewer

dropped_by: any defeated slime in depth 18 and below

royal skewer.description: a jagged, razor-sharp barb from one of the most powerful slimes on cradle. once removed from the slime it grows brittle and eventually turns to dust.
royal skewer.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 . despite its description, this material does not expire after being obtained.

schemer scrap.name: schemer scrap
schemer scrap.description: a fragment of a blueprint used by gremlin schemers to construct their mechanical monstrosities. with enough of them and a little tape, you could construct a metal death machine yourself!
schemer scrap.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-12-12 .

scrap metal.name: scrap metal

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 1 and below

scrap metal.description: a discarded piece of metal that could be easily repurposed.
scrap metal.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

shadow dust.name: shadow dust
shadow dust.description: a portion of special food favored by battle sprites who gain their powers using forbidden, dark arts.
shadow dust.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

shadow mote.name: shadow mote
shadow mote.description: a morsel of special food favored by battle sprites who gain their powers using forbidden, dark arts.
shadow mote.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

shadow orb.name: shadow orb
shadow orb.description: a lustrous sphere of special food favored by battle sprites who gain their powers using forbidden, dark arts.
shadow orb.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

shadow star.name: shadow star
shadow star.description: a perfect crystalization of special food favored by battle sprites who gain their powers using forbidden, dark arts.
shadow star.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

shadow steel.name: shadow steel

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 18 and below

shadow steel.description: an extraordinary metal imbued with the creeping darkness of the underworld.
shadow steel.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

shadow stone.name: shadow stone
shadow stone.description: a concentrated lump of special food favored by battle sprites who gain their powers using forbidden, dark arts.
shadow stone.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

sharp fang.name: sharp fang

dropped_by: any defeated beast or slime in depth 1 and below

sharp fang.description: a particularly pointy premolar. or maybe it's a canine?
sharp fang.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

silver coil.name: silver coil

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 13 and below

silver coil.description: an electromagnetic mechanism made of an extremely conductive metal.
silver coil.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

silverweave.name: silverweave

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 18 and below

silverweave.description: a length of understated cloth intricately interwoven with precious metal. it's tastefully ostentatious, yet prudently protective.
silverweave.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

sinful brew.name: sinful brew

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 4 and below

sinful brew.description: a noxious drink favored by all fiends. despite its prevalence, it's officially outlawed in the underworld as a marketing ploy.
sinful brew.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

snarbostuffing.name: snarbostuffing
snarbostuffing.description: a dense, fluffy stuffing made from a special polymer that doesn't break down when exposed to the cursed fur of the snarbolax. it's perfect for creating trophies or adorable, cursed, stuffed toys.
snarbostuffing.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-12-12 .

soul dust.name: soul dust

dropped_by: any defeated undead in depth 8 and below

soul dust.description: the dust of the departed. whether it still retains any shred of a soul remains to be seen.
soul dust.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

spark plug.name: spark plug

dropped_by: any defeated construct or gremlin in depth 1 and below

spark plug.description: a small, mechanical component that really gets a construct's motor running.
spark plug.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

spoiled treat.name: spoiled treat
spoiled treat.description: a terrible tummy ache awaits those who partake in the fetid fare of winterfest past.
spoiled treat.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-12-11 during the third winterfest.

standard supply pack.name: standard supply pack
standard supply pack.description: a package of useful supplies valued by spiral knight research teams working deep within the clockworks. this compact pack has enough supplies to last a few knights one week.
standard supply pack.notes: this material was introduced with release 2012-02-22 . it seems to function as a story item, keeping players mindful of the world around them.

static clinger.name: static clinger

dropped_by: any defeated shock themed monster in depth 4 and below

static clinger.description: a latent strand of energy that floats throughout the clockworks, frequently catching onto hair and clothing. it's too large to properly distill into energy, but it's very useful in alchemy.
static clinger.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

storm chaser.name: storm chaser
storm chaser.description: a rare elemental found hovering in storm clouds. their electric hum creates an almost melodic choir that can be faintly heard between thunderclaps.
storm chaser.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched. its name was changed from spark of life to storm chaser with release 2013-07-30 .

sun silver.name: sun silver

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 18 and below

sun silver.description: an extraordinary metal imbued with the radiant light of the sky.
sun silver.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

swordstone.name: swordstone

dropped_by: any defeated construct in depth 4 and below

swordstone.description: a fine chunk of natural rock perfect for honing the edge of a deadly blade.
swordstone.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

thunderball.name: thunderball

dropped_by: any defeated shock themed monster in depth 13 and below

thunderball.description: an orb of pure electricity that is typically found at the bottom of the sea of all places.
thunderball.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

torchstone.name: torchstone

dropped_by: any defeated fire themed monster in depth 8 and below

torchstone.description: a small rock that smolders constantly. gremlins patrolling the cooling chambers occasionally use them as a hand warmer.
torchstone.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

toxic core.name: toxic core

dropped_by: any defeated poison themed monster in depth 8 and below

toxic core.description: a solid, pulsating lump that oozes with vitriol.
toxic core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

trojan horseshoe.name: trojan horseshoe

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 13 and below

trojan horseshoe.description: a jet black horseshoe. it appears relatively harmless.
trojan horseshoe.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

unstable core.name: unstable core

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 8 and below

unstable core.description: a hazardous hunk pulled from a fulminating foe. it hums menacingly.
unstable core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

vile seal.name: vile seal

dropped_by: any defeated fiend or undead in depth 1 and below

vile seal.description: a small scrap of ancient parchment imbued with dark magicks. it is believed that they grant power to those with evil souls.
vile seal.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

volcanic iron.name: volcanic iron

dropped_by: any defeated gremlin in depth 18 and below

volcanic iron.description: an extraordinary metal imbued with the blazing energy of magma.
volcanic iron.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

volt oil.name: volt oil

dropped_by: any defeated shock themed monster in depth 8 and below

volt oil.description: a slippery substance that always holds a slight electrical charge.
volt oil.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

warp dust.name: warp dust

dropped_by: any defeated beast in depth 13 and below

warp dust.description: a light dust left behind when a creature travels at unbelievable speed. it tastes great in dip.
warp dust.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

waste module.name: waste module

dropped_by: any defeated poison themed monster in depth 4 and below

waste module.description: a small receptable that once collected refuse from a device built to wreak havoc. now, it just plain reeks.
waste module.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched.

wicked idol.name: wicked idol

dropped_by: any defeated fiend in depth 8 and below

wicked idol.description: one of many statuettes carried by fiends as a means of gaining favor with their dark gods. the most valuable ones are 'mibe' (mint in box of evil)
wicked idol.notes: this material was introduced with release 2013-07-30 .

wicked whisker.name: wicked whisker
wicked whisker.description: a whisker won from the great kat tribe hero, margrel. they were traditionally worn as a ward against owlite magics but now represent the triumph of goodly magical arts over evil.
wicked whisker.notes: this material was introduced 17 september 2014 during the fourth kataclysmic confrontation .

zee core.name: zee core
zee core.description: a pungent mass pulled from a slimy assailant. its aroma is an excellent remedy for insomnia, as well as nighttime sniffling and sneezing.
zee core.notes: this material has been available since release 2011-04-04 when the game officially launched. it was reintroduced with release 2017-05-11 .

forge_spark.name: Forge Spark

dropped_by: Construct, Gremlin, Fire, and Shock themed levels
Treasure Boxes

forge_spark.description: A small wisp of the essential spirit of technology and progress. It is of extreme value to the Spiral Order. Take it to the Token Trader in the Haven Bazaar!
forge_spark.notes: Used to purchase Construct and Tech-themed items from Brinks.

grim_spark.name: Grim Spark

dropped_by: Undead, Fiend, and Poison themed levels
Treasure Boxes

grim_spark.description: A small wisp of the essential spirit of darkness and decay. It is of extreme value to the Spiral Order. Take it to the Token Trader in the Haven Bazaar!
grim_spark.notes: Used to purchase Undead and Dark-themed items from Brinks.

primal_spark.name: Primal Spark

dropped_by: Slime, Beast, and Freeze themed levels
Treasure Boxes

primal_spark.description: A small wisp of the essential spirit of nature. It is of extreme value to the Spiral Order. Take it to the Token Trader in the Haven Bazaar!
primal_spark.notes: Used to purchase Nature and Beast-themed items from Brinks.

almirian_seal.name: Almirian Seal

dropped_by: Lord Vanaduke
Darkfire Vanaduke

almirian_seal.description: A seal from the lost kingdom of Almire, proof that the keeper braved the ever-burning abyss known as the Firestorm Citadel.
almirian_seal.notes: Boss Token. Quantity depends on depth entered (1-4 tokens).

bark_module.name: Bark Module

dropped_by: Roarmulus Twins
Red Roarmulus Twins

bark_module.description: A special circuit board developed by gremlin engineers designed to enhance both the bark and bite of the Roarmulus Twins.
bark_module.notes: Boss Token. Dropped in the Clockworks.

frumious_fang.name: Frumious Fang

dropped_by: Snarbolax
Rabid Snarbolax

frumious_fang.description: A long, razor-sharp tooth plucked from the mouth of a Snarbolax; a memento representing a Knight's triumph over the monstrous stalker of the Gloaming Wildwoods.
frumious_fang.notes: Boss Token. Dropped in the Gloaming Wildwoods.

jelly_gem.name: Jelly Gem

dropped_by: Royal Jelly
Ice Queen

jelly_gem.description: Typically only carried by jelly royalty, these squishy gems are the official currency of the jelly kingdoms. Though highly prized by the slimes, most creatures simply eat them as they are quite delicious.
jelly_gem.notes: Boss Token. Dropped in the Frostfire Jubilee.

krogmo_coin.name: Krogmo Coin

dropped_by: Coliseum matches (Participation)
Coliseum matches (Victory)

krogmo_coin.description: A coin issued by the great King Krogmo to participants in his Coliseum. It can be exchanged for prizes from the Coliseum Rewards Supervisor.
krogmo_coin.notes: PvP Token. 1 for participation, 2 for victory. Can be bribed to Krogmo for extra rewards.

sour_candy.name: Sour Candy

dropped_by: Dark Harvest Festival Enemies
Grim Gourdlings

sour_candy.description: A piece of candy made to celebrate the Dark Harvest Festival. It can be exchanged in the Haven Town Square for various costume masks.
sour_candy.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of the Dark Harvest Festival. Used for Phantom Mask line.

sweet_candy.name: Sweet Candy

dropped_by: Dark Harvest Festival Enemies

sweet_candy.description: A piece of candy made to celebrate the Dark Harvest Festival. It can be exchanged in the Haven Town Square for various costume masks.
sweet_candy.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of the Dark Harvest Festival. Used for Spookat Mask line.

hard_candy.name: Hard Candy

dropped_by: Dark Harvest Festival Enemies

hard_candy.description: A piece of candy made to celebrate the Dark Harvest Festival. It can be exchanged in the Haven Town Square for various costume masks.
hard_candy.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of the Dark Harvest Festival. Used for Frankenzom Mask line.

brittle_candy.name: Brittle Candy

dropped_by: Grim Gourdlings (from Grim Scarabs)

brittle_candy.description: A piece of candy made to celebrate the Dark Harvest Festival. It can be exchanged in the Haven Town Square for various costume masks.
brittle_candy.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of the Dark Harvest Festival. Used for Scarab Mask line.

chewy_candy.name: Chewy Candy

dropped_by: Dark Harvest Festival Enemies

chewy_candy.description: A piece of candy made to celebrate the Dark Harvest Festival. It can be exchanged in the Haven Town Square for various costume masks.
chewy_candy.notes: Event Token introduced in 2025. Expires at the end of the Dark Harvest Festival.

sticky_candy.name: Sticky Candy

dropped_by: Dark Harvest Festival Enemies

sticky_candy.description: A piece of candy made to celebrate the Dark Harvest Festival. It can be exchanged in the Haven Town Square for various costume masks.
sticky_candy.notes: Event Token introduced in 2025. Expires at the end of the Dark Harvest Festival.

blue_winter_wish.name: Blue Winter Wish

dropped_by: Save Winterfest! Event Mission

blue_winter_wish.description: A tiny wishlist created by one of the children of Emberlight. It signifies that you fulfilled their holiday wishes and saved Winterfest.
blue_winter_wish.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of Winterfest. Used for Round Santy Hat line.

red_winter_wish.name: Red Winter Wish

dropped_by: Save Winterfest! Event Mission

red_winter_wish.description: A tiny wishlist created by one of the children of Emberlight. It signifies that you fulfilled their holiday wishes and saved Winterfest.
red_winter_wish.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of Winterfest. Used for Santy Sallet line.

white_winter_wish.name: White Winter Wish

dropped_by: Save Winterfest! Event Mission

white_winter_wish.description: A tiny wishlist created by one of the children of Emberlight. It signifies that you fulfilled their holiday wishes and saved Winterfest.
white_winter_wish.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of Winterfest. Used for Tailed Santy Hat line.

green_winter_wish.name: Green Winter Wish

dropped_by: Save Winterfest! Event Mission

green_winter_wish.description: A tiny wishlist created by one of the children of Emberlight. It signifies that you fulfilled their holiday wishes and saved Winterfest.
green_winter_wish.notes: Event Token. Expires at the end of Winterfest. Used for Santy Pith Hat line.

frozen_winterfest_wishlist.name: Frozen Winterfest Wishlist

dropped_by: Mysterious Rooms (Winterfest Arcade)

frozen_winterfest_wishlist.description: A tiny wishlist created by one of the children of Emberlight. It signifies that you fulfilled their holiday wishes and saved Winterfest.
frozen_winterfest_wishlist.notes: Event Token. Found in Mysterious Rooms during Winterfest.

apocrean_sigil.name: Apocrean Sigil

dropped_by: Apocrea Event Bosses
Event Missions

apocrean_sigil.description: An otherworldly brand placed upon a soul claimed by the mysterious Apocrea. Removing it allows the captured soul to escape imprisonment within the terrible realm of the Apocrea.
apocrean_sigil.notes: Event Token. Used to purchase items from Apocrea vendors.

ancient_page.name: Ancient Page

dropped_by: Black Kats
Prestige Missions: That Mold, Black Magic Bad Luck and Brimstone, Purr Terror

ancient_page.description: A crumbling page torn from a long-forgotten grimoire of the Moorcroft Manor archives. The indecipherable language written upon it glows faintly in the dark as if containing power in its very words.
ancient_page.notes: Event Token (Kataclysmic Confrontation). Recovered from Black Kats or prestige missions.

cake_slice.name: Cake Slice

dropped_by: Creep Cakes (Cake Nests)
Prestige Missions: Total Caketastrophe!, A Gremlin in Knead
Bulky (Vendor)

cake_slice.description: A delicious slice of cake from the Caketastrophe event. It can be traded for special costume helmets or Anniversary Prize Boxes.
cake_slice.notes: Event Token (Caketastrophe). Expires after the event. Found in 'cake nests' in the Arcade.

black kat hat.name: black kat hat
black kat hat.description: a cursed mask created from a black kat. it is said to bring the wearer great misfortune.

industrial hard hat.name: industrial hard hat
industrial hard hat.description: normally worn by construction workers, this hat doesn't offer much defense but grants the wearer high stun resistance.
industrial hard hat.notes: this item was only available via purchase for 600 crowns in the haven bazaar during beta.

aero fin.name: aero fin
aero fin.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the top of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.

aggro aura.name: aggro aura
aggro aura.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aggro aura.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : depends on aura. use the control+f keyboard function and search for "prismatic" to find prismatic auras.

amethyst aura.name: amethyst aura
amethyst aura.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst aura.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : depends on aura. use the control+f keyboard function and search for "prismatic" to find prismatic auras.

amethyst bolted vee.name: amethyst bolted vee
amethyst bolted vee.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to select . accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst bolted vee.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the bolted vee is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon prismatic bolted vees were accidentally added to the supply depot for 0 energy during the 4th flash sale in february 2014. [1] after release 2026-02-04 it changed to the new helmet brow slot.

amethyst bomb bandolier.name: amethyst bomb bandolier
amethyst bomb bandolier.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst bomb bandolier.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : the slits on the undersides of the bombs. this item is often called a "bomb bundle" in release notes. [1]

amethyst com unit.name: amethyst com unit
amethyst com unit.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the side of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst com unit.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : the center slit where it attaches to the ear. the com unit is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon for a short time, the citrine com unit was colored in an odd manner , involving colors from the opal pattern. this was promptly fixed. [1] the npc kora wears a com unit. so do many miscellaneous npcs around the clockworks and haven.

amethyst crest.name: amethyst crest
amethyst crest.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst crest.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this type of crest usually signifies an affiliation, achievement, and/or rank. most are shaped like generic shields. the " dev crest " is a staff exclusive crest that looks like the dev deadzone , a staff-exclusive shield. the proto crest looks very similar to the green ward . for a short while, the ancient crest was colored like the polar crest.

amethyst crown.name: amethyst crown
amethyst crown.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to select . accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst crown.notes: the naming convention is "x crown," where "x" is the defining part of the name. prismatic aspect(s) : none. after release 2026-02-04 it changed to the new helmet brow slot.

amethyst dapper combo.name: amethyst dapper combo
amethyst dapper combo.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst dapper combo.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this accessory is animated. smoke comes out of the pipe. the npcs armero and impostoclaus seem to have the dapper combo. the impostocube monster wears a dapper combo. sometimes this monster drops a dapper combo or other related accessories. the impostoclaus hat costume has a modified, built-in dapper combo. this item is a combination of a mustache and a pipe .

amethyst disciple wings.name: amethyst disciple wings
amethyst disciple wings.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst disciple wings.notes: the naming convention is "x disciple wings," where "x" is the defining part of the name. prismatic aspect(s) : none. these wings flap and stretch occasionally. flapping is audible .

amethyst glasses.name: amethyst glasses
amethyst glasses.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst glasses.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the npc bechamel wears glasses.

amethyst headband.name: amethyst headband
amethyst headband.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the top of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst headband.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the headband is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npc kora wears a headband.

amethyst helm-mounted display.name: amethyst helm-mounted display
amethyst helm-mounted display.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst helm-mounted display.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : the inner circle. the helm-mounted display is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npc barrus wears a modified helm-mounted display. this item is often called a "power scanner" in release notes. [1] this name and this item's appearance possibly allude to the scouters in dragon ball z , which are used to scan power levels.

amethyst maid headband.name: amethyst maid headband
amethyst maid headband.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the top of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst maid headband.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the maid headband is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon this item is similar to a real-world nursing cap .

amethyst mecha wings.name: amethyst mecha wings
amethyst mecha wings.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the side of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst mecha wings.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : a slit on the section that attaches to the ear. the mecha wings is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npc armero wears mecha wings. the heavenly iron helm has some modified mecha wings on it.

amethyst parrying blade.name: amethyst parrying blade
amethyst parrying blade.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst parrying blade.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this accessory tends to flop around if the player moves. history 500 parrying blades (cool, dusky, fancy, heavy, and regal) were given away via codes during "black friday" promotions from various mmo communities in starting 2011.35 25 november 2011 . divine, prismatic, and volcanic were originally available in one of the featured auctions . military and toasty were released 2012.11 18 april 2012 , when they were added (along with round shades ) to the iron lockbox . for a short time, the citrine parrying blade had the proper icon, but was named and colored as a turquoise parrying blade. this was fixed with release 2015-11-24 .

amethyst plume.name: amethyst plume
amethyst plume.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst plume.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the npc biscotti wears a regal plume. the mighty cobalt helm , azure guardian helm , and most winged helms have plumes. the "pig tails" on tailed helms look a lot like plumes.

amethyst ribbon.name: amethyst ribbon
amethyst ribbon.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst ribbon.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the ribbon is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npcs kozma and bechamel wear ribbons.

amethyst round shades.name: amethyst round shades
amethyst round shades.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst round shades.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this item was introduced some time late in the year 2011, originally available from the featured auction house . later, along with parrying blades , several styles were added to the iron lockbox with release 2012-04-18 . the npcs kora and impostoclaus wear modified round shades. the impostoclaus hat has modified, built-in round shades.

amethyst scarf.name: amethyst scarf
amethyst scarf.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
amethyst scarf.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. many npcs wear scarves during winterfest . the ghostmane stalker gremlin always wears a scarf. several of the higher star armor items in the shade armor line have a built-in scarf. these are animated, flapping in the wind.

aquamarine helm-mounted display.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : the inner circle. the helm-mounted display is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npc barrus wears a modified helm-mounted display. this item is often called a "power scanner" in release notes. [1] this name and this item's appearance possibly allude to the scouters in dragon ball z , which are used to scan power levels.

aquamarine maid headband.name: aquamarine maid headband
aquamarine maid headband.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the top of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine maid headband.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the maid headband is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon this item is similar to a real-world nursing cap .

aquamarine mecha wings.name: aquamarine mecha wings
aquamarine mecha wings.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the side of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine mecha wings.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : a slit on the section that attaches to the ear. the mecha wings is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npc armero wears mecha wings. the heavenly iron helm has some modified mecha wings on it.

aquamarine parrying blade.name: aquamarine parrying blade
aquamarine parrying blade.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine parrying blade.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this accessory tends to flop around if the player moves. history 500 parrying blades (cool, dusky, fancy, heavy, and regal) were given away via codes during "black friday" promotions from various mmo communities in starting 2011.35 25 november 2011 . divine, prismatic, and volcanic were originally available in one of the featured auctions . military and toasty were released 2012.11 18 april 2012 , when they were added (along with round shades ) to the iron lockbox . for a short time, the citrine parrying blade had the proper icon, but was named and colored as a turquoise parrying blade. this was fixed with release 2015-11-24 .

aquamarine plume.name: aquamarine plume
aquamarine plume.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine plume.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the npc biscotti wears a regal plume. the mighty cobalt helm , azure guardian helm , and most winged helms have plumes. the "pig tails" on tailed helms look a lot like plumes.

aquamarine ribbon.name: aquamarine ribbon
aquamarine ribbon.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine ribbon.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the ribbon is grouped with other items. available in standard color patterns , they are options for accessorizing when creating a new knight: bolted vee com unit headband helm-mounted display maid headband mecha wings ribbon the npcs kozma and bechamel wear ribbons.

aquamarine round shades.name: aquamarine round shades
aquamarine round shades.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the front of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine round shades.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this item was introduced some time late in the year 2011, originally available from the featured auction house . later, along with parrying blades , several styles were added to the iron lockbox with release 2012-04-18 . the npcs kora and impostoclaus wear modified round shades. the impostoclaus hat has modified, built-in round shades.

aquamarine scarf.name: aquamarine scarf
aquamarine scarf.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine scarf.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. many npcs wear scarves during winterfest . the ghostmane stalker gremlin always wears a scarf. several of the higher star armor items in the shade armor line have a built-in scarf. these are animated, flapping in the wind.

aquamarine side blade.name: aquamarine side blade
aquamarine side blade.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine side blade.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this accessory tends to flop around if the player moves.

aquamarine toupee.name: aquamarine toupee
aquamarine toupee.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the top of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine toupee.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the npc impostoclaus wears a modified toupee with a santy hat. the impostocube monster wears a toupee. sometimes this monster drops a toupee or other related accessories when defeated. the toupee can also be used in the helmet brow slot if available.

aquamarine vertical vents.name: aquamarine vertical vents
aquamarine vertical vents.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the side of select helmets. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine vertical vents.notes: certain vertical vents are a rare drop from mewkats (style unknown). prismatic aspect(s) : none.

aquamarine wings.name: aquamarine wings
aquamarine wings.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
aquamarine wings.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. the winged spiral warden wears divine wings. sometimes called "gen1 wings" or "cardboard wings" by players.

arcane halo.name: arcane halo

chroma whelp monster pocket.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. since most monster pocket monsters are infant versions of the "normal" monster , it is implied that love puppies (and possibly other gun puppy models) are capable of reproducing without being built by gremlins or other machine-savy beings, a possible exception to the rule that constructs cannot reproduce. the pouch has the monster symbol (a.k.a stratum symbol) of the type of the monster it carries on the front.

cool dragon wings.name: cool dragon wings
cool dragon wings.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
cool dragon wings.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this accessory is animated. the wings will flap and stretch on occasion. flapping is audible .

cool ear gears.name: cool ear gears

cool exhaust pipes.name: cool exhaust pipes
cool exhaust pipes.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the back of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
cool exhaust pipes.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : none. this accessory is animated. they constantly shudder and expel exhaust .

cool extension cord.name: cool extension cord
cool extension cord.description: a ticket for an accessory that can be attached to the rear of select armors. accessory tickets can be redeemed at the accessorizor in the haven bazaar.
cool extension cord.notes: prismatic aspect(s) : the sparks around the plug. it appears to be a thrashing power cord with a 3-pronged plug . this movement is very similar to the cat tail accessory.
